Package config

Class AppConfig

java.lang.Object
config.AppConfig
public final class AppConfig extends Object
File-backed configuration for the UAV/WSN simulator.

Reads config.properties from the working directory so users can tweak simulation parameters and swap strategies without touching — or even opening — any Java source file.

Falls back to built-in defaults when the file is absent, so the application always starts cleanly.

What belongs here

  • Environment parameters — field size, node count, drone range, etc. (all simulation.* keys in config.properties).
  • Intelligence registry — the mapping from short string key to a factory that creates a fresh UAVIntelligence instance.

What does NOT belong here

Algorithm-specific parameters (GA population size, ACO evaporation rate, planning thresholds, …) live in their own config class (e.g. GaAcoConfig). Each algorithm owns its configuration; this class only knows about the simulation environment.

Adding a new intelligence strategy — three steps

  1. Implement UAVIntelligence.
  2. Add one line to REGISTRY: 
    REGISTRY.put("mykey", sim -> new MyIntelligence(sim));
  3. Add "mykey" to run.intelligences in config.properties.
No other code needs to change.

Typical usage

   AppConfig cfg = AppConfig.load();
   SimulationRunner runner = new SimulationRunner(cfg.buildSimulationConfig());
   if (cfg.getSeed() != AppConfig.NO_SEED) runner.withSeed(cfg.getSeed());
   List<UAVIntelligence> intelligences = cfg.buildIntelligences();

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    CONFIG_FILE
    Default config file name, looked up relative to the working directory.
    static final long
    NO_SEED
    Sentinel value meaning "no fixed seed — generate a fresh one per run".
    static final Map<String,Function<SimulationConfig,UAVIntelligence>>
    REGISTRY

  • Field Details

    • NO_SEED

      public static final long NO_SEED
      Sentinel value meaning "no fixed seed — generate a fresh one per run".
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • CONFIG_FILE

      public static final String CONFIG_FILE
      Default config file name, looked up relative to the working directory.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • REGISTRY

      public static final Map<String,Function<SimulationConfig,UAVIntelligence>> REGISTRY

  • Method Details

    • load

      public static AppConfig load()
      Loads configuration from config.properties in the working directory. Falls back to built-in defaults if the file is absent.
      Returns:
      a fully initialised AppConfig instance

    • load

      public static AppConfig load(String filePath)
      Loads configuration from the specified file path. Useful for testing or for specifying an alternate config location.
      Parameters:
      filePath - path to the .properties file
      Returns:
      a fully initialised AppConfig instance

    • buildSimulationConfig

      public SimulationConfig buildSimulationConfig()
      Builds a SimulationConfig from the simulation.* properties.

      Users adjust these values in config.properties; no Java changes needed to change field dimensions, node counts, drone parameters, etc.

    • buildIntelligences

      public List<UAVIntelligence> buildIntelligences()
      Builds the list of UAVIntelligence instances specified by run.intelligences.

      Each comma-separated key is looked up in REGISTRY. Unknown keys are logged and skipped rather than throwing, so a typo doesn't crash the whole application.

      Returns:
      ordered list of fresh intelligence instances
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no valid intelligence keys are found

    • buildIntelligences

      public List<UAVIntelligence> buildIntelligences(SimulationConfig simCfg)
      Builds intelligence instances using the provided SimulationConfig instead of the one read from config.properties.

      Use this overload when the user has configured a custom run via LaunchDialog and you want to apply those settings to the intelligence strategies without modifying the properties file.

      Parameters:
      simCfg - the simulation configuration to pass to each intelligence
      Returns:
      ordered list of fresh intelligence instances
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no valid intelligence keys are found

    • isHeadless

      public boolean isHeadless()
      Returns true if run.headless=true in the config. When headless, no GUI window is opened and output goes to stdout only.

    • getSeed

      public long getSeed()
      Returns the fixed random seed, or NO_SEED if none is set. A blank or missing run.seed means "generate a fresh seed per run".

    • getIntelligenceKeys

      public List<String> getIntelligenceKeys()
      Returns the configured intelligence keys from run.intelligences, filtered to only keys that are actually present in REGISTRY.

      Unknown keys are skipped (with a warning) so the panel count in the GUI always matches the number of simulations that will actually run.

    • registeredKeys

      public static List<String> registeredKeys()
      Returns all keys currently registered in the intelligence registry. These are the values you can put in run.intelligences.
